hero.description: I’m a software engineer with a degree in programming. In my day-to-day work, I develop Building Management Systems (BMS) using .NET, ASP.NET , C#, Blazor, DevExpress, PostgreSQL, and JavaScript. Outside of work, I build modern web applications with Next.js, React, TypeScript, and Node.js.

caseStudies.heading: Projects
caseStudies.description: A concise overview of projects where clean interfaces, practical features, and sound technical decisions mattered most.

budgetapp.label: Personal finance
budgetapp.title: Financial App
budgetapp.description: A personal finance tracker built with Next.js that lets users track expenses and income, import transactions from ING CSV files, and review everything in a dashboard.

catfacts.label: API and frontend
catfacts.title: Cat & Facts
catfacts.description: A responsive app fetching cat images and facts from a custom API, focused on simplicity and smooth interactions.

solarixo.label: Company website
solarixo.title: LurniFlow
solarixo.description: An app that turns what the user learns during a reading session into flashcards, helping reinforce the most important information in a more active way.

bms.label: Building automation
bms.title: BMS Platform
bms.description: A commercial Building Management Systems case focused on installation controls, data monitoring, and operator-facing workflows.

qrReader.label: Backend service
qrReader.title: QR Code Reader Service
qrReader.description: A Python service for reading QR codes from provided images, designed for integration with larger workflows and automated data processing.

photoExif.label: Backend service
photoExif.title: Photo EXIF Reader Service
photoExif.description: A Python service for extracting EXIF metadata from photos, used to read technical image details and prepare them for downstream processing.

bms.solution: Use this section to explain the architecture, chosen technologies, implementation decisions, and how the BMS problem was solved.
bms.outcome: Here you can add the final outcome, user impact, process improvements, or other concrete project results for the BMS platform.

qrReader.problem: You can describe the need for automated QR code extraction from images and how this service fits into a broader business workflow.
qrReader.solution: Use this section to explain the Python service design, image-processing flow, data validation, and integration points with the rest of the system.
qrReader.outcome: Here you can describe the rollout result, time savings, reduced manual work, or other benefits gained from QR automation.

photoExif.problem: You can describe the need to extract EXIF metadata and why technical photo information mattered in this workflow.

past.title: Past
past.text: I first discovered programming at 16 — it started as a hobby, but quickly turned into a passion. The ability to build something from nothing using just code captivated me early on. Over time, I sharpened my skills by building websites, experimenting with different technologies, and pushing myself to solve increasingly complex problems.

present.title: Present
present.text: Nowadays, I’m working as a Fullstack Developer, focusing on the development of Building Management Systems (BMS) for applications like heat pump control and building automation. My daily stack includes C#, Blazor, Javascript, .NET, PostreSQL, MQTT — on my free time, I continuously improve my skills with React, Next.js, Javascript and Typescript. I actively contribute to internal tools and open-source side projects, always aiming to deliver clean, scalable, and maintainable code.

future.title: Future
future.text: I’m driven by constant learning and challenges that push me out of my comfort zone. Looking ahead, I see myself deepening my expertise in full-stack development, exploring cloud-native systems, and contributing to products that solve real-world problems. My goal is simple: to create software that’s not just functional, but impactful.

project1.description: BudgetApp is a clean web app built in Next.js+ TypeScript, providing payouts in a list format interactions by user and category. Built-in demo allows sharing without logging in.

project2.description: Cat Generator is a clean, responsive web app built with Next.js and TypeScript. It fetches random cat images and fun facts using a custom REST API, with optional breed filtering. The interface is styled using Tailwind CSS and enhanced with smooth Motion One animations. The project is fully deployed on Vercel and ready to explore — no login required.

project3.description: The project focuses on modern design, clear content structure, and responsiveness, showcasing the company's offerings, collaboration process, and contact information in an accessible format. The site is designed as an example of a professional promotional website for local businesses in the renewable energy sector.

project4.description: A commercial Building Management Systems project focused on building controls, data monitoring, and operator workflows. My work here centers on stability, clear interfaces, and shipping features that are directly useful in day-to-day operations.
